input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS.

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS development)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **Why:** Provides iOS SDKs, simulators, and build tools
- **Version discovery:** Check [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) for the minimum supported Xcode version. Generally, use the latest stable Xcode that your macOS supports.
- **Post-install:**
  ```bash
  xcode-select --install          # installs command-line tools
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept # accept license non-interactively
  ```

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — download the latest stable **SDK** (not just Runtime)
- **Version discovery:**
  ```bash
  # See which MAUI workload ships with which SDK:
  dotnet workload search maui
  ```
- The .NET 8+ SDK includes the `maui` workload. Install it:
  ```bash
  sudo dotnet workload install maui
  ```

## 3. Android SDK, NDK & JDK

The MAUI workload installs Android dependencies automatically, but you can also manage them explicitly:

- **JDK:** The MAUI workload bundles Microsoft's OpenJDK. No separate install needed.
- **Android SDK:** Installed via the workload into `~/Library/Android/sdk` (or a path you configure). To manage manually, use [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) or the standalone [command-line tools](https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only).
- **Version discovery:** Check `dotnet workload list` output and the [MAUI system requirements docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation) for required Android API levels.

## 4. (Optional) IDE

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extensions | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** (discontinued) | Use VS Code instead |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) — has MAUI support |

## 5. Verify everything works

```bash
# 1. Check SDK
dotnet --version
dotnet workload list          # should show "maui" installed

# 2. Check Xcode
xcodebuild -version           # prints Xcode + Build version
xcrun simctl list devices     # lists available iOS simulators

# 3. Check Android
dotnet android sdk list       # or: $HOME/Library/Android/sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --list

# 4. Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

dotnet build -f net8.0-ios          # iOS build (succeeds = Xcode OK)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android      # Android build (succeeds = Android SDK + JDK OK)

# 5. Run on simulators/emulators
dotnet run -f net8.0-ios --project . # launches in iOS Simulator
dotnet run -f net8.0-android         # launches in Android emulator (create one first via Android Studio or AVD manager)
```

## Quick-reference install sequence

```bash
# Step 1 — Xcode (from App Store, then):
xcode-select --install
sudo xcodebuild -license accept

# Step 2 — .NET SDK (after downloading .pkg from dot.net):
dotnet workload install maui

# Step 3 — Verify:
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui && cd HelloMaui
dotnet build -f net8.0-android && dotnet build -f net8.0-ios
```

Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` (or later) if you installed a newer SDK. The target framework must match your installed SDK version.
